response.justification: The candidate demonstrates extensive experience as a full-stack engineer with over 10 years of relevant work, including roles that align closely with the primary stack (TypeScript, Node.js, React) and responsibilities. Their work on AI-agent infrastructure and developer tooling, as well as contributions to scalable systems handling millions of requests, highlight strong technical depth. However, their primary experience in Dlang and non-primary stacks (e.g., Swift, PHP) is less relevant to the role, and some recent roles are very short-term, which may raise concerns about stability.
strengths: Deep expertise in full-stack development with TypeScript, Node.js, and React, matching the primary stack requirements.
Proven track record in designing and optimizing scalable systems, including handling millions of HTTP requests and AI-agent infrastructure.
Strong focus on test-driven development, clean code, and collaboration with product/design teams, as evidenced by roles at multiple companies.
concerns: Primary stack experience is strong but secondary stacks (e.g., Dlang, Swift, PHP) dominate their resume, raising questions about long-term specialization.
Multiple short-term roles (e.g., 2 months at re:cinq, 14 months at Optilyz) and very brief stints (e.g., 6 months at WatchUp) may indicate job-hopping or instability.
Lack of explicit mention of Nest.js, Vitest, or Kotlin/Java for backend services in recent roles, which are core to the job description.

key_factors.factor: Experience with scalable systems and AI-agent infrastructure
Job stability and relevance of recent roles
